student_name: Eliane Portela Nogueira Lima de Brito
hub_subtitle: Business English for a doctor rebuilding her professional voice

lesson.menu_title: The Pitch — Presenting Your Case with Impact
lesson.menu_desc: Cleft sentences and emphasis (What we offer is... / It is X that...) to put the spotlight on your strongest point when you pitch -- 31 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 13 -- The Pitch
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Eliane Portela | Aula 13 -- The Pitch: Presenting Your Case with Impact

listenings.text: Hi Eliane, it is your pitch coach. One idea before tomorrow: it is not your slides that win the room, it is your first sentence. What investors remember is the one thing that makes you different, so lead with your differentiator. Do not list ten features. What matters is the single pain point you solve and the traction that proves it. When they ask about money, be clear: what they want to see is the return on investment and the upside. And tailor every line to the person in front of you, because a pitch that resonates with one investor may fall flat with another. Remember: what closes a round is not a perfect pitch deck, it is a compelling story that people believe.

Hi team, it is Eliane. Let me recap how we pitch tomorrow. What we lead with is our differentiator: it is our technology that lowers the cost per cycle. The reason we open there is simple, that is the pain point patients feel most. Then we show traction, the pilot and one strong use case, because what an investor wants is proof, not promises. When we reach the numbers, what we highlight is the return on investment and the upside. I have tailored the pitch deck to James, so it resonates with what he cares about. If we do this well, the standout in the room will be us, and we walk out with his buy-in.
